schema_note: Each exercise has backward-compatible 'text' field plus structured 'components' with full/lean variants for the exercise component assembly system.

exercises.name: Who Without Roles
exercises.text: If you removed all your roles, all your titles, all the labels you use to define yourself, who would you be? Not your job, your relationships, your achievements. Underneath all of that, who is the you that's always been there? This isn't about finding an answer. It's about sitting with the question. Let it open something in you.

bridge.full: Try this before you move on. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.
bridge.lean: Try this before you move on.

intro.full: This is Who Without Roles. Hold the question. Do not answer it. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.
intro.lean: This is Who Without Roles. Hold the question. Do not answer it.

integration.lean: Before you move on, the question stays with you. One breath at a time.

exercises.name: What Matters Most
exercises.text: If you knew you only had one year left to live, what would matter most to you? Not what you think should matter. What actually matters. What would you do more of? What would you let go of? This question cuts through all the noise and reveals what's really important. Listen to what arises.

bridge.full: Pause here for a moment. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is What Matters Most. Hold the question. Do not answer it. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.

exercises.name: Repeating Patterns
exercises.text: What pattern keeps showing up in your life? Maybe in relationships, in work, in how you treat yourself. What do you keep doing even though you say you want to change? Patterns exist for a reason. They served you once, protected you somehow. Can you look at this pattern with curiosity instead of judgment? What is it trying to protect you from?

bridge.full: Put the book down. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Repeating Patterns. Let it sit. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.
intro.lean: This is Repeating Patterns. Let it sit.

exercises.name: Unmet Needs
exercises.text: What need has gone unmet in your life? Maybe for a long time. Not wants or preferences, but real needs. Connection? Safety? Recognition? Freedom? Rest? Your unmet needs don't go away when you ignore them. They show up in other ways, often as suffering. What need is asking for your attention right now?

bridge.full: Before you keep reading. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Unmet Needs. The discomfort is the data. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.

exercises.name: Inner Wisdom
exercises.text: There's a part of you that knows what you need, what's true for you, what's right for you. It's not the loud, anxious voice. It's quieter, steadier, deeper. Can you hear it right now? What is your inner wisdom trying to tell you? You don't have to act on it yet. Just listen. Just acknowledge that you know more than you think you know.

bridge.full: Stop here. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Inner Wisdom. Let it sit. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.

exercises.name: Fear Exploration
exercises.text: What are you most afraid of? Not spiders or heights. The deeper fears. Failure? Rejection? Being alone? Being seen? Most of our lives are shaped by these fears, often unconsciously. What would change if you acknowledged your fear without letting it control you? Fear isn't the enemy. Unconscious fear is. Bring it into the light.

bridge.full: This one is for your body. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.
bridge.lean: This one is for your body.

exercises.name: Authentic Self
exercises.text: When do you feel most like yourself? Not the version of you that you think you should be. The real you. What are you doing? Who are you with? How does it feel in your body? Your authentic self is always there, but sometimes buried under expectations and conditioning. Where can you feel them right now?

bridge.full: Do this one out loud. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

intro.full: This is Authentic Self. Hold the question. Do not answer it. You do not need to believe that. Just try it.

exercises.name: What Am I Avoiding
exercises.text: What have you been avoiding? A conversation? A decision? A feeling? A truth you don't want to face? Avoidance takes enormous energy. The thing you're avoiding doesn't go away. It waits. It grows. What would happen if you turned toward it instead of away? Not to fix it or solve it. Just to acknowledge it. To say, I see you.

bridge.full: Close the book for this. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Permission I Need
exercises.text: What permission do you need to give yourself? Permission to rest? To say no? To want what you want? To change your mind? To be imperfect? We often wait for someone else to give us permission, but that person never comes. You are the only one who can free yourself. What permission are you waiting for?

bridge.full: Just thirty seconds. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: My Medicine
exercises.text: What is your medicine? What do you bring to the world that only you can bring? Not your job title. Not what you're good at. But what healing, what light, what unique perspective or presence do you offer? Everyone has medicine. Yours might be your humor, your steadiness, your ability to see beauty. What's yours?

bridge.full: Your body needs this one. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Shadow Recognition
exercises.text: What parts of yourself have you disowned or pushed away? Maybe your anger, your neediness, your ambition, your vulnerability. These shadow parts don't disappear. They leak out sideways. What if these parts aren't bad? What if they're just human? What if integrating them would actually make you more whole? Can you make space for what you've rejected?

bridge.full: This is the practice. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Unlived Life
exercises.text: What life have you not lived? What dreams did you put aside? What path did you not take? This isn't about regret. It's about acknowledging the road not traveled. Sometimes we need to grieve the lives we didn't choose before we can fully embrace the one we have. What unlived life needs your acknowledgment?

bridge.full: Right now. Not later. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: Inherited Beliefs
exercises.text: What beliefs did you inherit from your family or culture that you've never questioned? Beliefs about success, about worthiness, about what a good life looks like. These beliefs run deep. They feel like truth, but they're just stories someone else wrote. Which inherited beliefs are you ready to examine? Which ones still serve you, and which ones don't?

exercises.name: Where I Compromise
exercises.text: Where do you compromise yourself? Where do you say yes when you mean no? Where do you shrink, perform, or pretend to be someone you're not? This isn't about judging yourself. We all do this. But awareness is the first step. Where are you out of alignment with your own truth? What would it cost to come back into alignment? What would it give you?

exercises.name: My Resistance
exercises.text: What are you most resistant to right now? Change? Vulnerability? Letting go? Success? Resistance isn't the enemy. It's protection. It's your psyche saying this feels dangerous. Can you get curious about your resistance instead of fighting it? What is it protecting you from? What old wound does it think is still fresh?

exercises.name: What I'm Becoming
exercises.text: You're not the same person you were a year ago, five years ago. You're always becoming something. What are you becoming right now? Not who you think you should become. Who you actually are becoming, whether you intend it or not. Can you feel it? Can you sense the direction of your evolution? Are you growing toward something or away from something?

exercises.name: Grief I'm Holding
exercises.text: What grief are you holding that you haven't fully processed? Loss of a person, a dream, a version of yourself, a time in your life. Unmetabolized grief weighs you down. It colors everything. What loss needs your attention? What needs to be mourned so it can finally be released? Grief isn't something to fix. It's something to honor.

exercises.name: My Judgments
exercises.text: What do you most judge in others? Not little annoyances. The things that really trigger you. Here's the uncomfortable truth: what we judge most harshly in others is often what we can't accept in ourselves. Your judgments are mirrors. What are they showing you about yourself? This isn't about making yourself wrong. It's about getting free.

exercises.name: What I'm Loyal To
exercises.text: What are you loyal to? Not what you say you value, but what you actually give your time, energy, and attention to. Your loyalties might surprise you. Maybe you're loyal to productivity over presence. To others' needs over your own. To old pain over new possibility. Your loyalties shape your life. Are they serving you? Do you want to redirect any of them?

exercises.name: The Story I Tell
exercises.text: What's the story you tell about yourself? The narrative that explains who you are and why your life is the way it is. I'm the responsible one. I always struggle with this. I never get what I want. These stories become self-fulfilling prophecies. What story are you telling? Is it true? Is it the only possible story? What would a different story unlock?

exercises.name: My Envy
exercises.text: Who or what do you envy? Envy is uncomfortable, but it's also information. It points to what you want but haven't admitted you want, or what you've forbidden yourself to have. Your envy knows things about your deepest desires. What is it telling you? Not so you can judge yourself for wanting. So you can understand what your soul is hungry for.

exercises.name: Where I'm Stuck
exercises.text: Where do you feel stuck in your life right now? In a relationship, a job, a pattern, a way of being. Stuckness usually means you're afraid of what's on the other side of movement. What would you have to face if you moved? What would you have to let go of? What would you have to become? Sometimes staying stuck feels safer than stepping into the unknown.

exercises.name: My Resentment
exercises.text: What resentment are you carrying? Who or what are you angry at? Resentment is often anger that wasn't expressed or boundaries that weren't set. It's corrosive. It poisons you more than the person or situation you resent. What boundary needs to be set? What truth needs to be spoken? What do you need to forgive, not for them, but for you?

exercises.name: What I'm Protecting
exercises.text: What are you protecting by staying the way you are? Your defenses, your patterns, your ways of coping, they're all protecting something. Maybe your heart. Maybe your sense of control. Maybe an old wound. What needs so much protection? Is the threat still real, or are you protecting yourself from ghosts? What would safety feel like if it didn't require all this armor?

bridge.full: Right now. Not later. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: The Life I Want
exercises.text: If you could create any life, with no limitations, no obligations, no one judging you, what would it look like? Not what sounds good or impressive. What would actually make you feel alive? What would nourish you? This isn't a fantasy exercise. This is your soul showing you what it needs. Even if you can't have the whole vision, what pieces of it can you bring into your current life?

exercises.name: My Addictions
exercises.text: What are you addicted to? Not just substances. Busyness, drama, perfection, people-pleasing, your phone, achievement. What do you use to avoid feeling what you don't want to feel? Addiction is about numbing, escaping, controlling. What are you trying to escape from? What feeling are you avoiding? Awareness of addiction doesn't make it go away, but it's the beginning of freedom.

exercises.name: Who I Blame
exercises.text: Who or what do you blame for your life being the way it is? Your parents, your circumstances, your partner, your past, the system, your body. Blame feels powerful but it actually makes you powerless. It keeps you a victim. What would happen if you stopped blaming, not because they were right, but because you want your power back? What becomes possible when you take full responsibility for your life from this point forward?

bridge.full: Put the book down. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: My Scarcity
exercises.text: Where do you operate from scarcity? Not enough time, not enough money, not enough love, not enough opportunities. Scarcity thinking creates scarcity reality. It makes you grip, hoard, compete, panic. What would shift if you believed there was enough? Not pretending, but genuinely exploring what enough might feel like. Where could you trust abundance instead of fearing scarcity?

bridge.full: Before you keep reading. Not to fix anything. Just to give yourself a different input for a moment.

exercises.name: The Child I Was
exercises.text: Think about yourself as a child. Seven, eight, nine years old. What did that child need that they didn't get? What did they experience that was too much to handle? That child is still in you. Their needs didn't go away. Their wounds didn't heal just because you grew up. What does your inner child need from you now? Can you give them the care, the safety, the love they needed then?

exercises.name: My Enough
exercises.text: What would be enough for you to feel okay about yourself? Enough success, enough money, enough recognition, enough love. Most of us are chasing a finish line that keeps moving. What if you already are enough? What if you always have been? What would change if you truly believed that? How would you live differently if you weren't trying to prove your worth anymore?

exercises.name: What I'm Denying
exercises.text: What truth are you denying? About yourself, your relationships, your situation. Denial is a powerful defense, but it requires constant energy to maintain. What reality are you not letting yourself see? What would you have to deal with if you acknowledged it? Denial protects you from pain in the short term but creates more pain in the long term. What truth is ready to be seen?

exercises.name: My Legacy
exercises.text: When you're gone, what do you want to have mattered? Not accomplishments or achievements necessarily. But how do you want people to have felt around you? What do you want to have stood for? What impact do you want your life to have had? Your legacy isn't something you create at the end. You're creating it right now with every choice. Is the life you're living creating the legacy you want to leave?
